title: Women of the Supreme Court
titleZh: 最高法院的女性大法官

q.en: What important case did Ruth Bader Ginsburg argue before the Supreme Court in 1971?
q.zh: 露丝·巴德·金斯伯格在1971年之前为最高法院辩护的重要案件是什么？

explain.en: The book says: 'For Ruth Bader Ginsburg, 1971 was an important year. She was a lawyer for a case going to the U.S. Supreme Court... This important case was called Reed v. Reed.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'对露丝·巴德·金斯伯格来说，1971年是重要的一年。她是一起送交美国最高法院案件的律师……这起重要案件叫作里德诉里德案。'

q.en: Which amendment to the Constitution did Ruth argue also protected women from discrimination?
q.zh: 露丝主张宪法中的哪一条修正案同样保护女性免受歧视？

options.en: The Nineteenth Amendment according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 第十九修正案（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Ruth argued 'that the Fourteenth Amendment to the Constitution also protected women from discrimination.' The amendment was added in 1868 after the Civil War.
explain.zh: 露丝主张'宪法第十四修正案同样保护女性免受歧视'。该修正案于1868年南北战争后加入宪法。

q.en: Who became the first female Supreme Court justice, and in what year was she nominated?
q.zh: 谁成为最高法院首位女性大法官，她是在哪一年获提名的？

explain.en: The book states: 'Ten years after Reed v. Reed, Sandra Day O'Connor became the Court's first female justice.' She was nominated in 1981.
explain.zh: 书中写道：'里德诉里德案十年后，桑德拉·戴·奥康纳成为最高法院首位女性大法官。'她于1981年获提名。

q.en: Where did Sandra Day O'Connor grow up?
q.zh: 桑德拉·戴·奥康纳在哪里长大？

options.zh: 在亚利桑那州的一个牧场

options.en: In New York City according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在纽约市（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says: 'Sandra grew up on a ranch in Arizona. She rode horses and was treated like one of the boys.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'桑德拉在亚利桑那州的一个牧场长大。她骑马，被当作男孩子一样对待。'

q.en: What was special about Elena Kagan's government job before she joined the Court?
q.zh: 埃琳娜·卡根在进入最高法院之前，她的政府职务有何特别之处？

options.en: She was the first Latina justice according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 她是首位拉丁裔大法官（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: She was the first woman to be the top lawyer for Supreme Court cases
options.zh: 她是负责最高法院案件的顶级律师中的第一位女性

options.en: She grew up on a ranch

explain.en: The book says she 'became the top lawyer in the U.S. government for cases brought to the Supreme Court. She was the first woman in that role.'
explain.zh: 书中说她'成为美国政府中负责送交最高法院案件的顶级律师。她是担任这一职务的第一位女性。'

q.en: How many female justices does the book say serve on the Supreme Court today?
q.zh: 书中说当今最高法院共有几位女性大法官？

explain.en: The book states: 'The four women of the Supreme Court come from different ethnic groups and backgrounds.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'最高法院的四位女性来自不同的族裔和背景。'

q.en: According to the book, how many justices does Congress require, and how are they appointed?
q.zh: 根据本书，国会规定最高法院需有几名大法官，他们如何产生？

options.en: Nine; appointed for life, with a new one only after retirement or death
options.zh: 九名；终身任职，只有在退休或去世后才提名新法官

options.en: Six; chosen by the president alone according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 六名；仅由总统选定（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says: 'Congress requires the Supreme Court to have nine justices. The justices are appointed for life. The only time a president nominates a new justice is after one has retired or died.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'国会规定最高法院须有九名大法官。大法官为终身任职。总统只有在有人退休或去世后才能提名新大法官。'

q.en: What did Sandra Day O'Connor's tie-breaking votes help protect?
q.zh: 桑德拉·戴·奥康纳的关键一票有助于保护什么？

options.en: The right to free speech only according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 仅言论自由权（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The rights of women and the right to privacy

explain.en: The book says: 'Sandra's tie-breaking votes helped protect the rights of women and the right to privacy.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'桑德拉的关键一票有助于保护女性权利与隐私权。'

q.en: How many senators voted 'yes' to confirm Sonia Sotomayor?
q.zh: 有多少名参议员投'赞成'票确认索尼娅·索托马约尔？

explain.en: The book says: 'Because sixty-eight senators voted "yes," she became the first Latina and the third woman on the Court.' (31 voted against.)
explain.zh: 书中写道：'因为有六十八名参议员投了赞成票，她成为最高法院首位拉丁裔女性及第三位女性大法官。'（31人反对）

q.en: What did Ruth Bader Ginsburg write the Court's explanation about?
q.zh: 露丝·巴德·金斯伯格撰写了最高法院关于哪方面的解释？

options.en: Why women cannot be denied entrance to any public school
options.zh: 为何不能拒绝女性进入任何公立学校

options.en: Why the death penalty is wrong
Why the Court needs nine justices according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为何法院需要九名大法官（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says she 'wrote the Court's explanation of why women cannot be denied entrance to any public school.'
explain.zh: 书中写道她'撰写了最高法院的解释，说明为何不能拒绝女性进入任何公立学校。'

prompt.en: Imagine you are a young girl in 1981 when Sandra Day O'Connor became the first female justice. Write a short letter to her describing what her achievement means to you.
prompt.zh: 想象你是1981年桑德拉·戴·奥康纳成为首位女性大法官时的一个小女孩。写一封短信给她，描述她的成就对你意味着什么。

en: Mention why having a woman on the Supreme Court was important
Use details from the book such as O'Connor growing up on a ranch or opening her own law office
Express your own hopes for the future
zh: 说明最高法院中有女性为何重要
运用书中细节，如奥康纳在牧场长大或自己开办律师事务所
